pattern: Every top-left-to-bottom-right path must cross each anti-diagonal exactly once, so a diagonal where at most one cell lies on some path is a single-flip bottleneck that severs every path at once.
hints: Every right/down path from the top-left to the bottom-right corner must pass through exactly one cell of each anti-diagonal (cells where row+col is constant).

Compute, from the start, which cells are reachable through 1s, and separately, from the end, which cells can still reach the finish through 1s; a cell lying on some full path is reachable in both directions.

If any interior diagonal (excluding the corners themselves) has zero or one such on-path cell, flipping it (or nothing, if already zero) disconnects everything; if every interior diagonal has two or more, no single flip can ever work.

edge_inputs.description: single cell grid, start equals end, no flip can disconnect it from itself
grid already disconnected, zero flips needed
single row, every interior cell is a one-cell bottleneck
single column, every interior cell is a one-cell bottleneck
fully open small grid where every diagonal has multiple crossing cells
two parallel routes around a hole, exactly two on-path cells at the tightest diagonal
a narrow corridor forces a single-cell bottleneck partway through a larger grid

stress.description: random square-ish grid of 0/1 cells with the two corners forced to 1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    dim = max(2, min(size, 60))
    rows = dim
    cols = dim
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.75 else 0 for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    grid[0][0] = 1
    grid[rows - 1][cols - 1] = 1
    return [grid]
